name: Bill's Basic Bread Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Use a hearty bread for best results; avoid breads like Wonder Bread.
2. Cut or tear the bread into cubes and place in a bowl.
3. In a blender or by hand, mix together the eggs, milk, sugar, salt, vanilla, and nutmeg.
4. Pour the liquid mixture over the bread cubes and let soak for at least one hour, or cover and refrigerate overnight.
5. Grease a 3-quart oven-proof casserole dish with butter. If any butter remains after greasing, place it in the bottom of the dish before adding the remaining ingredients.
6. Pour all ingredients into the prepared casserole dish. The milk/egg mixture should be about an inch above the bread cubes.
7. Place the casserole in a pan of very hot water, which should come about two-thirds up the side of the dish.
8. Bake in a pre-heated oven at 325°F until set, about 30-40 minutes. The pudding will rise like a souffle but may collapse when removed from the oven.
9. Serve warm or cold with or without your favorite sauce or whipped cream.
NOTE:
There should be enough bread to fill the casserole dish two-thirds full. The liquid mixture should be approximately an inch above the bread mixture in the dish.
